questions.meaning: Words given at the back of the chapter
chewed or bit upon noisily; munched
questions.sample_sentence: I champed the delicious idiappam served by Sastha Catering Services.

questions.meaning: grazing; eating grass or plants by biting off the tops
questions.sample_sentence: I saw a ravenous cow cropping the thick grass we had grown just outside our house.

questions.meaning: moved downward; came down from a higher place
questions.sample_sentence: My music teacher descended and opened the door when I rang the doorbell at her house.

questions.meaning: covered with or full of ferns (a type of leafy plant)
questions.sample_sentence: There is an old abandoned ferny compound near my house in Chennai.

questions.meaning: listening attentively; paying attention to sounds
questions.sample_sentence: My Dhrupad teacher asks me to constantly be hearkening in the class to whatever he is demonstrating.

questions.meaning: poetic form of 'beneath'; under or below
questions.sample_sentence: The book that I was searching for was lying 'neath my bed.

questions.meaning: confused and puzzled; unable to understand something
questions.sample_sentence: My father created a course named Way2top for people perplexed with what career to choose.

questions.meaning: a ghost or spirit; something that appears to exist but has no physical reality
questions.sample_sentence: My cousin Prahlad says that there is nothing like a phantom that exists in this world.

questions.meaning: the horizontal piece at the bottom of a window or door frame
questions.sample_sentence: The window sills in my house are made large enough to allow us to sit on them.

questions.meaning: past tense of 'smite'; struck or hit with force
questions.sample_sentence: The Swiggy delivery guy smote upon our door when no one opened the door in response to the doorbell.

questions.meaning: an archaic past tense of 'speak'; spoke
questions.sample_sentence: As Suvasana spake very rudely to me, I got angry with her.

questions.meaning: a metal loop that hangs from a horse's saddle to support the rider's foot
questions.sample_sentence: My feet couldn't reach the stirrup of the horse when I wanted to ride it during our vacation.

questions.meaning: grass and the surface layer of earth held together by its roots
questions.sample_sentence: My friend Yohann plays tennis on a special turf made for children.

questions.meaning: a small tower, especially one at the corner of a building or castle
questions.sample_sentence: The chimneys I saw during a factory visit looked similar to turrets in old British buildings.

questions.meaning: Other important words from the poem
lived or resided in a place
questions.sample_sentence: My cousin Vishwak dwelt in a small house near T. Nagar before his family moved to a bigger apartment.

questions.meaning: the quality of being still; absence of movement or sound; complete quiet
questions.sample_sentence: I love the stillness of Marina Beach early in the morning before the crowds arrive.

questions.meaning: moved suddenly and powerfully forward or upward, like a wave
questions.sample_sentence: The CSK fans surged towards the entrance of Chepauk when the gates opened for the IPL match.

questions.meaning: falling or moving suddenly and forcefully downward or forward
questions.sample_sentence: I watched the waterfall plunging into the rocks below during our family vacation to the hills.

questions.meaning: the quality of being shadowy; full of shadows; dark and indistinct
questions.sample_sentence: The shadowiness of the corridor near my Dhrupad class made me walk quickly to reach the well-lit music room.

questions.meaning: solitary; standing alone; without companions
questions.sample_sentence: I was the lone person practising mridangam in the music hall while everyone else had gone home.

questions.meaning: strange and frightening; mysterious in an unsettling way
questions.sample_sentence: The empty streets near Greams Road looked eerie during the late evening after all the shops had closed.

questions.meaning: crowding together in large numbers; gathering densely
questions.sample_sentence: People were thronging the streets of Mylapore during the temple festival, and we could barely move forward.

questions.meaning: repeating or reverberating sound; reflecting sound waves
questions.sample_sentence: My mridangam beats kept echoing through the empty hall at Sishya school during my practice session.

questions.meaning: illuminated by the light of the moon
questions.sample_sentence: We took Nandi for a walk along the moonlit terrace, and he looked so happy running around in the cool breeze.

questions.meaning: a large number of people or things; a multitude
questions.sample_sentence: A host of food stalls appeared near Chepauk on match day, selling everything from biryani to filter coffee.
